findings.evidence: `rg -n "abbrev point|point_apply|comp_point" code/TauCeti/Algebra/AlgebraicGroup/Trivial.lean` shows only `abbrev point` and no `point_apply`/plain inverse naturality lemma; Mathlib has `Algebra.ofId_apply`, so the missing API is exactly the unfolded body.
findings.fix: Make `point` an opaque non-exposed definition and add `@[simp] theorem point_apply (r : R) : point r = algebraMap R A r` plus the naturality lemma `φ.comp point = point`, analogous to `MultiplicativeGroup.comp_point`.
findings.issue: `point` is a public `abbrev`, exposing `Algebra.ofId` as its body instead of providing the characteristic API for the unique point.

findings.evidence: Line 94 proves `convPoint_eq_one (f : WithConv (R →ₐ[R] A)) : f = 1`, and `rg -n "\[simp\].*convPoint_eq_one|convPoint_eq_one"` finds no simp-tagged version.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to `convPoint_eq_one` (or provide an equivalent simp normal-form lemma) so arbitrary trivial-group points simplify to `1`.
findings.issue: `convPoint_eq_one` is the normal-form lemma for the trivial convolution group but is not tagged `@[simp]`.

summary: The exposed surface relies on reducible bodies for the main point and leaves the identity normal form out of simp, so downstream users do not get the promised trivial-points API without unfolding or manual rewrites.
